mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required by Cluster API to manage Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-crds.yaml

```

> Ensure you have the correct version of CRDs.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API controller
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API controller using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

```

> Install the controller in the kube-system namespace.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to define a new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Cluster API components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Look for pods with 'cluster-api' in their names.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will delete all managed clusters and associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-crds.yaml

```

> This command removes the Cluster API controller.
> This command removes the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster configurations. This step will upgrade the Cluster API controller and verify the installation afterward.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o json > clusters-backup.json

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup current cluster configurations.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or is not available in the specified version.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the infrastructure provider CRDs and controllers are installed and configured properly.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in kube-system are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system and resolve any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the API server is not reachable or is taking too long to respond.

**Fix:**
Check your network configuration and ensure that the Kubernetes API server is running and accessible.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers, improved error handling during cluster creation.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version 'v1alpha3', which is no longer supported.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to migrate your existing Cluster resources to the new API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your created clusters with their statuses as 'Provisioned' or 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
